stories.title: Self-Hosted Docker Topology Stand-Up
acceptance_criteria: docker compose config validates with zero errors and resolves all x-* env anchors onto the services that reference them.
After up -d, docker compose ps shows one running container per single-replica service and migrator shows Exited(0).
The named volumes pgdata, redisdata, uploads, rabbitmq_data, proxy_config, proxy_data, and the four logs_* volumes are created and bound.
depends_on ordering verified: stopping plane-db surfaces api reconnect in logs_api; a full restart returns the stack to all-running.
Stack uses pinned images postgres:15.7-alpine, valkey/valkey:7.2.11-alpine, rabbitmq:3.13.6-management-alpine, minio/minio:latest, and makeplane/plane-* pinned via APP_RELEASE.
tasks: Validate and bring up the full compose topology
Verify dependency ordering and restart resilience
Create the E1 tracking issue in Plane

stories.title: Reverse Proxy & Port-8090 Ingress
acceptance_criteria: curl -sf http://localhost:8090/ returns HTTP 200 and serves the Plane web app shell.
curl -sf http://localhost:8090/api/instances/ returns a JSON instance descriptor from the api service, confirming /api reaches api:8000.
Proxy published ports are exactly 8090->80 and 8453->443; no internal service port (8000, 9000, 5432, 6379, 5672) is host-published.
CORS preflight from origin http://localhost:8090 is accepted; an origin not in CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS is rejected.
proxy_config and proxy_data volumes persist the Caddy config and provisioned certs across a proxy restart.

stories.title: Environment-Driven Configuration & Secrets
acceptance_criteria: Running-container env and proxy/app logs show no Postgres/MinIO/RabbitMQ password or SECRET_KEY echoed in a request URL or access-log line.
Every secret/credential in plane.env is overridable and the override propagates to the consuming service (verified via api effective env).
DATABASE_URL, AMQP_URL, REDIS_URL resolve correctly both blank-defaulted and explicitly set.
WEBHOOK_ALLOWED_IPS/WEBHOOK_ALLOWED_HOSTS are applied: a non-allowlisted private-IP webhook is refused; an allowlisted one is permitted.
DEBUG=0 is the deployed default; DEBUG=1 is recognized as a non-production posture.
tasks: Prove no secret leakage (REQ-17.5 substrate guard)

stories.title: Durable Storage Tier (Postgres + MinIO + RabbitMQ + Redis)
acceptance_criteria: plane-db accepts connections (pg_isready), max_connections=1000 is set, and the plane database exists with Plane's migrated schema after migrator completes.
The MinIO uploads bucket exists and round-trips identical bytes via http://plane-minio:9000 with configured AWS credentials (content-addressed-blob contract).
RabbitMQ plane-mq is reachable on 5672 with vhost plane and worker/beat-worker connect and consume.
Redis/Valkey plane-redis responds to PING and live/api use it.
Volume-durability proof: a marker Postgres row and MinIO object survive docker compose down (no -v) then up -d.
tasks: Provision the four durable tiers on named volumes

stories.title: Database Migration & Version Upgrade
acceptance_criteria: On first up, migrator applies Plane's schema and exits 0; api starts cleanly with no relation-does-not-exist errors in logs_api.
Re-running with no schema change is a no-op migration (migrator exits 0, applies nothing new).
An APP_RELEASE bump pulls new images and the upgrade migration runs against existing pgdata without data loss (REQ-INFRA-004 marker row survives).
The companion .kiro migrations 001-008 are applied through a tracked runner with schema_migrations bookkeeping; double-apply is idempotent and produces all eight tables.
A deliberately broken migration causes migrator to exit non-zero and the app tier is held from advancing.
tasks: Execute and verify a version upgrade
Apply the companion eight-table .kiro migrations idempotently

stories.title: Backup & Restore of the Durable Tier
acceptance_criteria: A pg_dump of the plane database completes, is non-empty, and is restorable into a fresh Postgres instance.
A MinIO uploads bucket mirror reproduces every object byte-identical, verified by output_hash for evidence blobs.
Round-trip restore proof: seeded marker row + evidence object survive docker compose down -v then restore into fresh volumes and up -d.
Backup artifacts carry a recorded timestamp and integrity checksum; a corrupted backup is rejected by the restore procedure.
The restore runbook is documented end-to-end and retention (>= the 365-day audit-log window) is stated.
tasks: Document the restore runbook and retention

stories.title: Horizontal Scaling via Replicas
acceptance_criteria: Setting API_REPLICAS=2 and re-applying starts two api containers; docker compose ps shows both running and the proxy serves from either.
Setting WORKER_REPLICAS=2 starts two workers and RabbitMQ shows both consumers attached to the plane queues; tasks are distributed.
The stateful services remain at replicas:1 and are never scaled by a *_REPLICAS knob.
beat-worker defaults to one replica; scaling the worker tier does not duplicate scheduled (beat) tasks.
Scaling up then back down is graceful: surplus replicas stop cleanly and the remaining replica continues serving.
tasks: Record scaling envelope on the issue

stories.title: Health, Readiness & Liveness Verification
acceptance_criteria: A readiness script returns exit 0 only when all four backing stores are healthy, migrator exited 0, and http://localhost:8090/ + http://localhost:8090/api/instances/ both return 200/JSON.
Killing any single backing store flips the readiness check to FAIL and names that tier.
The api service reachable through the proxy returns a valid instance/health payload, confirming the REST surface downstream epics consume is live.
Webhook egress configuration (WEBHOOK_ALLOWED_IPS/WEBHOOK_ALLOWED_HOSTS) is in place so a later gate-decision webhook has a valid allowlist-checked target.
Readiness PASS is captured as the gating Evidence_Record before any downstream epic (workspace provisioning) is unblocked.
tasks: Verify the REST/Webhook surface is live and gate the downstream epic
